abstract: Detecting small objects in remote sensing imagery remains a significant challenge due to heavy background noise and the scarcity of pixel-level information, often leading to suboptimal performance by standard detection frameworks. To address these limitations, we propose LAR-YOLOv8, an enhanced detection algorithm derived from the YOLOv8 architecture. Our approach introduces three key innovations: First, we refine the feature extraction network by integrating a dual-branch attention mechanism to bolster local feature capture alongside a vision transformer block to maximize global feature representation. Second, we design an attention-guided bidirectional feature pyramid network (ABFPN) that utilizes a dynamic sparse attention mechanism. This structure efficiently extracts details from shallow layers and incorporates additional top-down paths to guide feature fusion across the network. Finally, we introduce the RIOU loss function to prevent gradient loss and ensure superior shape consistency between predicted and ground-truth bounding boxes. Comprehensive evaluations on the NWPU VHR-10, RSOD, and CARPK datasets demonstrate that LAR-YOLOv8 delivers substantial improvements in mAP and small-object detection accuracy while maintaining competitive model parameters and inference speeds. These results validate the effectiveness of our modifications for robust remote sensing applications.
